default_creds.context: Hardcoded default admin login printed in the official README quickstart and docs.maxkb.pro/quick_start/. Globally identical across every default-image deployment until the operator manually changes it. Note the literal trailing two dots in the password.

misconfig_patterns: Exposed on 8080 to the internet still using the shipped default admin / MaxKB@123.. credential (most common and highest-impact misconfig)
Default deployment with no reverse-proxy auth and no IP allowlist in front of the single image
Running a build < 2.4.0 exposing the tool-module sandbox-escape chain (CVE-2025-66419) to any authenticated/low-priv user
Cross-origin enabled on an API key (off by default) widening unauth API reach
Old builds storing unsalted MD5 password hashes, trivially crackable once the user table is read

response_markers: JSON body with top-level integer field "code" (e.g. 200) and "message" plus a "data" object — MaxKB's standard Django REST envelope
Unauthenticated calls to management routes return code/message envelope rather than HTML, distinguishing the API from the Vue SPA shell
Login page served at /ui/login with Vue app and document title containing 'MaxKB'
Server/X-Powered-By and routing handled by bundled nginx in front of the Django/uWSGI app on a single image
active_probe.false_positive_check: Do NOT match on generic strings ('chat','AI','knowledge base','RAG') — they collide with thousands of unrelated chatbots. Anchor on the conjunction of: (1) http.title or HTML containing the exact token 'MaxKB' (vendor coined, low collision), AND (2) the SPA route prefix '/ui/chat/' or '/ui/login' which is MaxKB-specific (not the generic '/chat'), AND (3) an active GET /api/application/profile returning the {code, message, data} JSON envelope. The 1Panel ecosystem also ships the 1Panel host-management panel (default port 80/443, distinct title '1Panel') and JumpServer (same FIT2CLOUD vendor, different favicon/title) — confirm the literal 'MaxKB' token and the /api/application/ route prefix to avoid attributing a sibling FIT2CLOUD product. Favicon-hash dorking is unreliable here: MaxKB has rebranded across the 1.x->2.x line, so a single favicon hash will both miss old builds and risk colliding with other Vue/Element-Plus default-favicon apps; prefer the title+route conjunction over favicon alone.

shodan_dorks.version: http.title:"MaxKB" http.html:"v1.9" (version surfaces in the SPA bundle/footer, not a server banner; confirm exact version at the active /doc or from JS bundle rather than relying on this dork)

deployment_tells: Single self-contained Docker image '1panel/maxkb' bundling Vue SPA + Django/uWSGI backend + nginx + PostgreSQL/pgvector + Python sandbox in one container
Listens on 8080 by default; web UI at http://host:8080/ui/, login at /ui/login
Volume mounts ~/.maxkb -> /var/lib/postgresql/data (DB lives inside the same container) and ~/.python-packages -> /opt/maxkb/app/sandbox/python-packages (tool-module sandbox)
Often co-deployed alongside the 1Panel host panel and other FIT2CLOUD products (JumpServer, 1Panel) on the same host — pivot indicator
Element-Plus / Vue front-end; drf-yasg-style API docs
Chinese-origin vendor (FIT2CLOUD, Hangzhou); docs bilingual (docs.maxkb.pro / maxkb.cn)
pivot_paths: GET /api/application/profile to confirm platform + enumerate published-application metadata without admin auth
If default creds unchanged: authenticate admin/MaxKB@123.. (READ-ONLY confirm only) -> model-management lists configured upstream LLM provider key references and knowledge-base inventory
Published-application public chat link /ui/chat/<access_token> exercises the RAG corpus unauthenticated (reveals ingested-document content via answers) where an app is published
Co-located 1Panel host panel / JumpServer on the same operator host as a lateral OSINT pivot (same FIT2CLOUD ecosystem)
API docs route enumerates the full route table for the running version, scoping which CVEs/GHSAs apply
vulnerabilities: CVE-2024-56137 (HIGH, CVSS 7.2, CWE-78 OS command injection): privileged user can execute arbitrary OS commands via custom scripts in the function-library module; affects < 1.9.0, fixed 1.9.0
CVE-2025-32383 (reverse shell / RCE): function-library module lets a privileged user create a reverse shell; sandbox-related
CVE-2025-64511 (SSRF, < 2.3.1): Python code in the tool module reaches internal network services (e.g. databases) despite the sandbox
CVE-2025-64703 (information leak, < 2.3.1): Python code in the tool module reads sensitive host information despite the sandbox
CVE-2025-66419 (HIGH, CVSS 8.8): tool-module sandbox escape -> privilege escalation under concurrent conditions; affects <= 2.3.1, fixed 2.4.0 (GHSA-f9qm-2pxq-fx6c)
GHSA-r3j3-j58q-rjpp (Moderate): Webhook Trigger Authentication Bypass (CWE-287/CWE-306) — missing auth on webhook trigger endpoint
GHSA-r8hf-mwwr-hxgc (High): SSRF bypass in OSS URL Fetch via URL-parsing discrepancy
GHSA-x9g5-j56j-4mfj (Low): Unauthenticated SSRF via Workflow Template Import
GHSA-6m4p-9wwc-4q5q (Low): SSRF bypass via DNS rebinding in OSS URL Fetch
GHSA-2jmj-gwvg-3gp2 (Low): Broken Access Control in OSS URL Fetch API
GHSA-q2qg-43vq-f2wv / GHSA-3rq5-pgm7-pvp4 / GHSA-462x-99gf-mp79 (Moderate): Stored XSS via iframe_render, html_rander markdown tags, and EchartsRander eval injection
GHSA-rr4r-7cj2-29vp (Low): CSV injection in Application Chat export
Historical (issue #970): Django JSONField SQL injection exposure (CVE-2024-42005 dependency)
auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: official README.md and docs.maxkb.pro/quick_start/ both ship the SAME quickstart: `docker run -d --name=maxkb --restart=always -p 8080:8080 -v ~/.maxkb:/var/lib/postgresql/data -v ~/.python-packages:/opt/maxkb/app/sandbox/python-packages 1panel/maxkb` and then instruct: log in at http://server_ip:8080 with default credentials user `admin` / pass `MaxKB@123..`, then 'Change the default password after logging in.' So the first-run posture is auth-on-default (a Django login gates the UI and the management API), NOT open-by-default. The load-bearing caveat: MaxKB ships a HARDCODED, globally-known shared default credential (admin / MaxKB@123..) baked into the image's bootstrap rather than generating a random one or forcing a setup wizard. An operator who runs the documented quickstart and exposes 8080 to the internet without changing the password is one credential-stuff away from full admin (model API keys, RAG corpus, agent flows). The thin 'login required' shell therefore degrades in practice to near-open for the not-uncommon default-password deployment. Reinforced by shipped-version auth gaps in primary advisories: GHSA-r3j3-j58q-rjpp 'Webhook Trigger Authentication Bypass' (CWE-287/CWE-306, missing auth on the webhook trigger endpoint) and GHSA-2jmj-gwvg-3gp2 'Broken Access Control in MaxKB OSS URL Fetch API' — both are routes that answered unauthenticated in released builds.
sensitive_assets: Upstream LLM provider API keys (OpenAI/Azure/Ollama/DeepSeek/Qwen/Claude model credentials configured under the model-management module) — recoverable on admin access
Ingested RAG knowledge-base documents and any PII/PHI/business-confidential content uploaded into them
Application/agent and workflow flow definitions (revealing internal business logic, prompts, tool wiring, MCP tool configs)
End-user conversation history / chat logs (chat_message records, exportable to CSV)
Raw vector store contents (PostgreSQL + pgvector embeddings of the ingested corpus)
Per-application API keys (application-prefixed bearer tokens) usable to drive the chat/completions endpoint
User accounts and unsalted MD5 password hashes (GHSA-2m4c-mcq5-q8xq) in older builds
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
